What are vector databases and why are they used in AI applications?

A vector database stores data as high-dimensional numerical vectors (embeddings) and enables extremely fast similarity search — finding the closest vectors to a query vector.

Why they matter for AI

Large language models convert text into embedding vectors. A vector database lets you search millions of documents by semantic meaning rather than exact keyword match, which powers features like RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).

import cohere
import numpy as np

co = cohere.Client('YOUR_API_KEY')
embeddings = co.embed(texts=['Vector databases are fast'], model='embed-english-v3.0')
vec = np.array(embeddings.embeddings[0])
print(f'Embedding dimension: {vec.shape[0]}')

Which vector databases are most popular right now?

The most widely adopted options are Pinecone (managed cloud service), Weaviate (open-source, GraphQL API), Qdrant (Rust-based, very fast), and pgvector (Postgres extension for teams already on PostgreSQL). For local/embedded use, ChromaDB and FAISS (by Meta) are popular in prototyping and research settings.
